notes: Curated benchmark database for AgentCollect audit reports. STRICT RULE: only recognized brands (McKinsey, Federal Reserve, AFP, NACHA, PwC, Deloitte, Atradius, Salesforce, NIST, Gartner, Forrester). Each stat has source_brand, source_url, year, verified flag, use_when guidance, and math_template for dollar-amount impact calculations. verified=true means the stat was confirmed via WebSearch citing the named source in the same sentence as the number. verified=false means TBD — we know the report exists or the stat is approximately right but need to re-verify the exact figure and URL.

payment_acceptance.stat: Only 26% of organizations now make B2B payments by check, a 7-point drop from 2022 — businesses are actively shifting away from paper.
payment_acceptance.source_full_name: AFP 2025 Digital Payments Survey (J.P. Morgan underwriter)
payment_acceptance.use_when: Prospect is still heavily check-based or forces customers to mail paper checks
payment_acceptance.math_template: If 74% of your peers have moved off checks and you still route invoices through the mail, you're adding 5-10 days of float PER invoice and lagging the AFP benchmark by a full survey cycle.

payment_acceptance.stat: 28% of organizations are already using faster/real-time payments for B2B settlement.
payment_acceptance.use_when: Prospect does not offer real-time, same-day ACH, or RTP rails
payment_acceptance.math_template: Nearly 1 in 3 of your direct competitors can already accept same-day funds. Every day you don't = a day of customer friction AND a day of your own cash sitting in float.

payment_acceptance.stat: Checks are still the #1 payment method accepted by US small businesses, followed by credit cards and cash — despite universal consumer card expectations.
payment_acceptance.source_full_name: Federal Reserve 2024 Report on Payments — Findings from the 2023 Small Business Credit Survey
payment_acceptance.use_when: Contextualizing why SMB customers still struggle with digital-only checkout
payment_acceptance.math_template: Your customers' finance teams are wired for check workflows. If you don't accept ACH + card AND provide a simple way to pay by check, you're forcing them to choose between convenience and compliance with their own AP process.

dso_benchmarks.stat: Global DSO has risen 5.7% over the past decade, from 47.3 days in 2015 to 50.0 days in 2024. North America alone is up 5.5%.
dso_benchmarks.use_when: Prospect's DSO is above 50 days or trending up
dso_benchmarks.math_template: Every 1 day of DSO on $X annual revenue = $X/365 locked in receivables. If you're at 65 days and the global median is 50, that's 15 × (revenue/365) in working capital you're financing for free.

dso_benchmarks.stat: Half of all B2B invoices issued by US companies are currently overdue. Overdue invoices are turned into cash an average of 20 days past due.
dso_benchmarks.source_full_name: Atradius Payment Practices Barometer — United States 2024
dso_benchmarks.use_when: Prospect has a large overdue bucket or slow-paying customers
dso_benchmarks.math_template: If 50% of your AR is overdue and the US average turnaround is DSO+20, every $1M in overdue invoices costs ~$1M × 20/365 × cost_of_capital in financing cost. On $10M overdue at 10% cost of capital = ~$55K in pure float cost per year.

dso_benchmarks.stat: The #1 cited reason for late B2B payments in the US is administrative inefficiencies in the customer's AP process.
dso_benchmarks.source_full_name: Atradius Payment Practices Barometer — United States 2024
dso_benchmarks.use_when: Prospect blames customers but their own billing UX is the friction
dso_benchmarks.math_template: If your customers cite 'admin inefficiency' as the reason they pay late, every extra click, every unclear invoice, every missing portal login is directly subtracting from your cash conversion.

dso_benchmarks.stat: Median DSO for B2B SaaS is 50 days (Cloud/IT Infrastructure segment). Top-quartile SaaS firms run DSO 15-25% below their industry average.
dso_benchmarks.source_full_name: State of B2B Payments 2024 (Upflow) — looking for a McKinsey/KPMG/Deloitte SaaS benchmark citation
dso_benchmarks.notes: Need first-party SaaS benchmark from KPMG/Bessemer/OpenView. Current source is a vendor blog.
dso_benchmarks.math_template: Top-quartile SaaS collects in ~40 days. If you're at 65 days, you're 25 days × (ARR/365) behind the top quartile in working capital efficiency.

payment_friction_cost.stat: 80%+ of executives say they've lost business because of a payment process miscommunication (2024).
payment_friction_cost.source_full_name: PYMNTS Intelligence — looking for original 2024 study citation
payment_friction_cost.notes: Seen in secondary coverage; need to track down original PYMNTS report
payment_friction_cost.use_when: Prospect has opaque billing or no clear payment options page
payment_friction_cost.math_template: If 8 in 10 executives have lost deals over payment confusion, every unclear invoice is a lost-deal risk. On a 20-deal-per-month pipeline, even a 5% friction-induced churn = 1 deal/month lost = $X/year.

payment_friction_cost.stat: US B2B companies write off an average of 8% of all B2B credit sales as bad debt.
payment_friction_cost.source_full_name: Atradius Payment Practices Barometer — United States 2024
payment_friction_cost.use_when: Always — this is a foundational pain point
payment_friction_cost.math_template: On $X in annual B2B credit sales, the US average write-off is X × 0.08. For a $50M company that's $4M/year being burned. Even a 25% improvement = $1M recovered.

payment_friction_cost.stat: Manual invoice processing costs $12-25 per invoice; automated best-in-class AP teams process at $1.42-$2.78 per invoice — a ~78% cost reduction.
payment_friction_cost.source_full_name: IOFM Benchmark (reported in Deloitte / Centime analyses) — need direct IOFM/APQC source
payment_friction_cost.notes: Need first-party APQC or IOFM benchmark report URL
payment_friction_cost.math_template: If you process N invoices/year manually at $15 each vs. $2.78 automated, savings = N × $12.22. At 50K invoices/year = $611K in pure process cost.

ar_team_size.stat: Finance departments can save up to 25,000 hours of avoidable rework per year by leveraging automation in daily A/R operations.
ar_team_size.source_full_name: Gartner Finance research (cited across HighRadius / Emagia analyses)
ar_team_size.notes: Need direct Gartner report URL — the 25K hours figure is widely cited but needs a primary-source link
ar_team_size.use_when: Prospect has a large manual AR team
ar_team_size.math_template: 25,000 hours × $45/hr fully loaded analyst cost = $1.125M per year in recoverable team capacity per Gartner's finding.

online_payment_adoption.stat: 69% of B2B buyers say it's absolutely critical or very important for vendors to offer self-service options.
online_payment_adoption.source_full_name: Salesforce State of the Connected Customer
online_payment_adoption.use_when: Prospect has no self-service portal, forces phone calls, or buries 'pay online' behind login walls
online_payment_adoption.math_template: 7 out of every 10 buyers consider self-service critical. If you force them to call to pay, you're actively violating the expectation of the majority of your own customers.

online_payment_adoption.stat: 82% of business buyers want the same experience when buying for work as they get buying for themselves.
online_payment_adoption.source_full_name: Salesforce State of the Connected Customer
online_payment_adoption.use_when: Prospect's billing UX is clearly more '1998 ERP' than '2024 Stripe checkout'
online_payment_adoption.math_template: If your customer can pay their Netflix in 3 clicks on their phone, but paying your invoice takes 7 steps and a desktop login, you're losing against an expectation set by Amazon, not your competitors.

small_business_payment_preferences.stat: 80% of small businesses reported challenges related to how they send and receive payments. Top complaints: fees and payments not arriving fast enough.
small_business_payment_preferences.source_full_name: Federal Reserve 2024 Report on Payments — Small Business Credit Survey
small_business_payment_preferences.math_template: 4 out of 5 of your SMB customers are already frustrated with B2B payments in general — per the Fed. You don't need to convince them there's a problem. You need to show you solve it.

small_business_payment_preferences.stat: Credit card processing fees are the most common payments challenge cited by small businesses, especially in retail, leisure and hospitality.
small_business_payment_preferences.source_full_name: Federal Reserve Bank of Boston — 2025 analysis of SBCS payments data
small_business_payment_preferences.use_when: Prospect serves SMBs that accept cards and are fee-sensitive
small_business_payment_preferences.math_template: If your SMB customers are already losing 2-3% to card fees, any additional friction on YOUR side (disputes, chargebacks, reversed payments) is intolerable.

after_hours_support.stat: TBD — specific stat on % of B2B vendors with 24/7 or extended-hours billing support
after_hours_support.notes: Looking for a J.D. Power, Gartner, or Forrester after-hours B2B support stat. Current proxy: Salesforce State of Service reports availability expectations.
after_hours_support.use_when: Prospect's billing line closes at 5pm local — mystery shop evidence

identity_verification_standards.stat: NIST SP 800-63-4 (finalized July 2025) defines three Identity Assurance Levels (IAL1-3) that financial services, healthcare, and regulated industries use as the baseline for identity proofing when handling sensitive transactions.
identity_verification_standards.source_full_name: NIST Special Publication 800-63-4 Digital Identity Guidelines
identity_verification_standards.use_when: Prospect handles card data, banking info, or takes payment over the phone without any identity check
identity_verification_standards.math_template: NIST 800-63 is the federal baseline. If your billing line takes a card number without ANY identity proofing step, you are not even at IAL1 — you're below the bar the federal government sets for 'any identity interaction at all.'

bad_debt_writeoff_rates.stat: Bad debts average 6% of all B2B credit sales across North America (USMCA).
bad_debt_writeoff_rates.source_full_name: Atradius Payment Practices Barometer — North America (USMCA) 2024
bad_debt_writeoff_rates.use_when: Calculating addressable bad-debt recovery opportunity across a multi-region prospect
bad_debt_writeoff_rates.math_template: 6% of B2B credit sales as bad debt is the NA benchmark. For $X in B2B revenue, your expected write-off is X × 0.06 — if you're above that, you have a collections problem; if you're at benchmark, AgentCollect can push you below it.

payment_method_friction.stat: B2B ACH payments grew 11.6% in 2024 to 7.3 billion payments — the fastest-growing segment of the ACH Network. Same-Day ACH B2B grew 50.5% in volume.
payment_method_friction.use_when: Prospect doesn't offer ACH or Same-Day ACH as a payment option
payment_method_friction.math_template: While B2B ACH grew 11.6% and Same-Day ACH B2B grew 50.5% last year, you're still sending wire instructions by email. Your customers are literally asking for the rail you don't support.

payment_method_friction.stat: 63% of organizations experienced attempted or actual check fraud in 2024 — making checks the most fraud-prone payment method.
payment_method_friction.source_full_name: AFP 2025 Payments Fraud and Control Survey
payment_method_friction.use_when: Prospect still relies heavily on checks
payment_method_friction.math_template: Nearly 2 in 3 companies had check fraud attempts in 2024 (AFP). Every invoice you force onto checks is a fraud surface you're choosing to own.

healthcare_practice_saas.stat: TBD — dental/vet/medical practice payment DSO benchmark
healthcare_practice_saas.notes: Looking for AMA, ADA, or AVMA practice management benchmark on AR/DSO

smb_phone_voip.stat: TBD — VoIP/UCaaS billing and churn benchmarks
smb_phone_voip.notes: Looking for Gartner UCaaS MQ churn/billing data

restaurant_pos.stat: Retail, leisure and hospitality businesses (including restaurants) are MORE likely than other industries to cite card processing fees as their top payment challenge.
restaurant_pos.source_full_name: Federal Reserve Bank of Boston analysis of Small Business Credit Survey payments data
restaurant_pos.math_template: Your restaurant customers are ALREADY the most fee-sensitive segment in the Fed data. Any added friction on your billing side compounds a pain they already feel every day.

vertical_saas_smb.stat: TBD — DSO + churn benchmark for vertical SaaS serving SMBs (Jobber, ServiceTitan, Procore)
vertical_saas_smb.notes: Looking for Bessemer Cloud Index or KeyBanc SaaS survey segmented by SMB vertical

field_service_saas.stat: TBD — field service SaaS customer payment behavior benchmark
field_service_saas.notes: Looking for Housecall Pro / ServiceTitan / Jobber aggregate payment data, or ASA (Aberdeen) field service report

need_followup: after_hours_support — no recognized-brand stat found in 40 min window
smb_phone_voip — need Gartner UCaaS billing data
vertical_saas_smb — need Bessemer / KeyBanc SaaS survey cut
ar_team_size — Gartner 25K hours figure needs primary URL
